title: quercus × hickelii
intro: Quercus × hickelii, also known as the Mexican white oak or Hickel's oak, is a hybrid of the Quercus obtusiloba and Quercus robur species, a type of deciduous tree native to Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high tannin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Quercus × hickelii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help lower cholesterol levels and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections.
